topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my phone, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my afternoon. My jaw tightened almost involuntarily as I stared at the profile picture: a familiar, sneering grin that instantly sent a cold knot tightening in my stomach. It was Mark. Mark, who used to enjoy making my life a little more difficult than necessary. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips, the kind that tasted like dust. I didn't even bother to tap the message. Just a low, frustrated groan rumbled in my chest. Why now? What did he want? I swiped the notification away, the screen momentarily illuminating the dull ache behind my eyes.

[story 2]

I was hunched over my textbook, the fluorescent lights of the library buzzing overhead, trying desperately to focus on the quadratic equations. Then, the phone vibrated. My breath hitched. It was Mark. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly. A wave of profound weariness washed over me, making my shoulders slump further down my back. I looked at the request, a slow, deliberate movement. A flicker of something unpleasant—a mix of resentment and sheer exhaustion—crossed my features before I finally dismissed it, letting the phone drop onto the table with a soft thud. It was pointless.

[story 3]

The email landed in my inbox, stark white against the dark background of my screen. It was from Mark. A familiar pang of irritation shot through me, making my hands clench into fists in my lap. I didn't move to open it immediately. I just sat there, staring at the blank wall across the room, the silence amplifying the thrumming tension inside me. Every muscle in my body felt weighted down, heavy with unspoken grievances. When I finally clicked on the message, the words were exactly what I expected: insincere and empty. I closed the window abruptly, the sound sharp in the quiet room.
